## North American Immersion
### Why attend an english language school in North America?
With a 4.8-star rating across 195 student reviews, choosing the right english language school defines your study abroad trip. Results matter. Our english language school operates accredited campuses in San Diego, California, Los Angeles, California, and Vancouver, British Columbia. As an institution recognized by the Commission on English Language Program Accreditation (CEA), we pair small class sizes with highly qualified teachers to build real fluency. Students from Brazil, Switzerland, South Korea, and dozens of other nations arrive every Tuesday to begin their studies. You will practice conversation naturally while living near beaches or busy downtown centers. Small groups mean your teachers know your name and your personal goals. The focus remains strictly on practical communication, pronunciation, and listening comprehension. We designed these locations to give you immediate access to local culture outside the classroom.

#### How do the summer camp course tracks differ?
The curriculum splits into two distinct paths. The General English track welcomes all proficiency levels, with participants starting new classes every Monday. This option covers all aspects of second language acquisition. Teachers focus heavily on speaking and listening confidence. For teenagers aiming higher, the Future Global Leaders track offers a specialized business and cross-cultural curriculum. This intensive option requires a minimum Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) level B2 to participate. It runs in three-week sessions starting July 1. Students in this advanced group tackle project-based assignments. They build leadership abilities alongside their language skills.

### What makes the English Plus Experience right for you?
The English Plus Experience combines structured language acquisition with practical, real-world application across our campus cities. Students who enroll in this program want more than traditional classroom instruction. They spend their mornings building core competencies in speaking, listening, reading, and writing. Afternoons shift to applied practice. You will engage directly with the local culture in San Diego, California, Los Angeles, California, or Vancouver, British Columbia. The curriculum aligns with the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) standards, accommodating learners from A1 beginners to C2 proficient speakers. Because our schools hold accreditation from the Accrediting Council for Continuing Education and Training (ACCET) in the United States and Languages Canada in the north, you receive high-quality academic oversight. This format accelerates fluency by forcing students out of their comfort zones. Real conversations with native speakers cement the grammar and vocabulary learned earlier in the day. The result is a balanced study schedule that builds confidence rapidly. It works.

## Alltägliche Sprachkenntnisse
### Wer sollte einen allgemeinen Englischkurs belegen?

word_from: A general english course is the standard starting point for students arriving in San Diego, Los Angeles, and Vancouver. The curriculum covers reading, writing, listening, and speaking while aligning directly with the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) levels A1 through C2. Students taking 20 or more hours per week typically advance one full CEFR level every 10 to 12 weeks. This progression depends on attending all classes and completing daily homework assignments. The primary focus remains on everyday communication rather than specialized academic vocabulary. If your goal is to travel confidently, speak with locals, or build a strong baseline before attempting advanced qualifications, this framework provides the right support. Yes. It sets the foundation. Kursteilnehmer mit 20 oder mehr Unterrichtsstunden pro Woche steigen typischerweise alle 10 bis 12 Wochen um ein komplettes CEFR-Niveau auf.
